title: Rabdosia coetsa

This family is further divided into many sub-families, but the specific classification of Rabdosia coetsa is not well-documented. More research is required to determine the precise classification of this plant species.

In traditional medicine, it is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. intro: Rabdosia coetsa, also known as "Donglingcao" in Chinese, is a type of medicinal plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exert antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The bioactive constituents of R. coetsa include diterpenoid alkaloids, such as rabdosia alkaloids A and B, as well as sesquiterpenoids and phenolic compounds. In traditional Chinese medicine, R. coetsa is typically prepared as a decoction or an extract, which is often used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
